events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes block/brick laying on an industrial site, rough-complete stage, with the condition "ladder reinforcement spacing exceeded." This means the horizontal joint reinforcement (ladder type) spacing in masonry exceeds the specified maximum spacing.

TMS 402/602 typically requires joint reinforcement at maximum spacing of 16 inches on center for certain conditions, or as specified by the engineer. Exceeding the spacing means the reinforcement is placed too far apart, reducing the structural capacity of the masonry wall to resist shrinkage cracking and provide required tensile reinforcement.

This is a non-compliant condition. The work is rough-complete, so it's installed but defective. Decision should be fail since it's non-compliant.

Let me estimate a value - if typical spacing is 16" o.c. and it's exceeded, maybe observed at 24" o.c. or similar. I'll put a reasonable estimate.

Percent complete for rough-complete block/brick laying - maybe 85%.

Risk: medium to high since it affects structural integrity. I'll say high given industrial setting.

Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant.
